flavorText: Thieves excel at speed, precision, and exploiting objects or opportunities in the moment.

features.description: You can use your bonus action for Use an Object, thieves' tools interactions, or a pickpocket attempt.
Climbing no longer costs extra movement, and your running jump distance increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.
You have advantage on Stealth checks when you move at no more than half your speed.
You can use scrolls, wands, and staves even if class, race, or level requirements would normally prevent it.
In the first round of combat, you can take two turns: one at your normal initiative and one at initiative minus 10.

thief-fast-hands.summary: Handle objects, tools, and interaction tricks faster than most characters could ever manage.
thief-fast-hands.description: Fast Hands turns the Thief into a battlefield opportunist who can exploit items, gadgets, and environmental interactions more efficiently than other rogues.

thief-second-story-work.summary: Climbing and urban movement become much easier, reinforcing the subclass’s classic nimble burglar fantasy.
thief-second-story-work.description: Second-Story Work is quiet but important. It changes how the rogue approaches vertical spaces, windows, roofs, and escape routes.

thief-supreme-sneak.summary: Slow, careful movement lets you push stealth beyond normal rogue competence.
thief-supreme-sneak.description: Supreme Sneak rewards patient infiltration and keeps the Thief excellent at the kind of cautious movement many dungeons actually demand.

thief-use-magic-device.summary: Magic items become easier for you to exploit even when they were not designed with you in mind.
thief-use-magic-device.description: Use Magic Device is a strong expression of the Thief fantasy: if there is a tool worth stealing, borrowing, or activating, this rogue finds a way to make it work.

thief-thiefs-reflexes.summary: Your reactions and battlefield timing become extraordinary, making you feel almost impossibly quick.
thief-thiefs-reflexes.description: Thief's Reflexes is the capstone-style speed payoff for a subclass built around movement, timing, and opportunism.
